segments.text: The Lord appeared to Abraham near the great trees of Mamre while he was sitting at the entrance to his tent in the heat of the day.
Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he hurried from the entrance of his tent to meet them and bowed low to the ground.
He said,
If I have found favor in your eyes, my lord, do not pass your servant by.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Let a little water be brought, and then you may all wash your feet and rest under this tree.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Let me get you something to eat, so you can be refreshed and then go on your way—now that you have come to your servant.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Very well, do as you say.
segments.speaker: The men

segments.text: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah.
Quick,
get three seahs of the finest flour and knead it and bake some bread.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Then he ran to the herd and selected a choice, tender calf and gave it to a servant, who hurried to prepare it.
He then brought some curds and milk and the calf that had been prepared, and set these before them. While they ate, he stood near them under a tree.
Where is your wife Sarah?
segments.speaker: The men

segments.text: There, in the tent.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Then one of them said,
I will surely return to you about this time next year, and Sarah your wife will have a son.
segments.speaker: One of the men

segments.text: Now Sarah was listening at the entrance to the tent, which was behind him.
Abraham and Sarah were already very old, and Sarah was past the age of childbearing.
So Sarah laughed to herself as she thought,
After I am worn out and my lord is old, will I now have this pleasure?
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: Then the Lord said to Abraham,
Why did Sarah laugh and say, 'Will I really have a child, now that I am old?'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Is anything too hard for the Lord? I will return to you at the appointed time next year, and Sarah will have a son.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Sarah was afraid, so she lied and said,
I did not laugh.
segments.speaker: Sarah

segments.text: But he said,
Yes, you did laugh.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When the men got up to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked along with them to see them on their way.
Then the Lord said,
Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Abraham will surely become a great and powerful nation, and all nations on earth will be blessed through him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: For I have chosen him, so that he will direct his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing what is right and just, so that the Lord will bring about for Abraham what he has promised him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then the Lord said,
The outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is so great and their sin so grievous
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: that I will go down and see if what they have done is as bad as the outcry that has reached me. If not, I will know.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The men turned away and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the Lord.
Then Abraham approached him and said:
Will you sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: What if there are fifty righteous people in the city? Will you really sweep it away and not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous people in it?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Far be it from you to do such a thing—to kill the righteous with the wicked, treating the righteous and the wicked alike. Far be it from you! Will not the Judge of all the earth do right?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: The Lord said,
If I find fifty righteous people in the city of Sodom, I will spare the whole place for their sake.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Abraham spoke up again:
Now that I have been so bold as to speak to the Lord, though I am nothing but dust and ashes,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: what if the number of the righteous is five less than fifty? Will you destroy the whole city for lack of five people?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: If I find forty-five there, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: What if only forty are found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: For the sake of forty, I will not do it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak. What if only thirty can be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I will not do it if I find thirty there.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Now that I have been so bold as to speak to the Lord, what if only twenty can be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: For the sake of twenty, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak just once more. What if only ten can be found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: For the sake of ten, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When the Lord had finished speaking with Abraham, he left, and Abraham returned home.
